Title: Characterization of the horse chestnut genome reveals the evolution of aescin and aesculin biosynthesis

Common_Genes.Applied_Species: Escherichia coli; in vitro enzyme system
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in seeds; transcribed BAHD within AcCluster I
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acetylates protoaescigenin and desacetylaescin I but with much lower conversion than AcBAHD6
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzyme assays with recombinant proteins from E. coli; LC–MS
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seeds, especially cotyledons near the testa
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AcBAHD3 acetylated protoaescigenin and desacetylaescin I in vitro. 2. Its activity was substantially weaker than AcBAHD6. 3. It is a supporting rather than dominant acyltransferase in escin Ia formation.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower catalytic efficiency than AcBAHD6 may limit engineering value
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core experimentally tested gene, but not the major determinant of escin Ia production
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Secondary acyltransferase candidate; less preferred than AcBAHD6 for engineering
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing CRA009101; transcriptome CRA009093; RNA-seq; WGCNA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes 2,3-oxidosqualene to the pentacyclic triterpene β-amyrin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms the core scaffold precursor of aescin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal anti-edema and venotonic activities; used clinically for chronic venous insufficiency, edema, and hemorrhoids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seeds, especially cotyledons near the testa for escin Ia/Ib
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables β-amyrin production as the initial dedicated precursor for aescin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in N. benthamiana; GC–MS/MS product identification; phylogenetic analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AcOSC6 produced β-amyrin in N. benthamiana. 2. Its seed-biased expression matches aescin accumulation. 3. It is a core scaffold-forming enzyme in the BAT BGC.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Located in AcCluster I, one of the duplicated BAT biosynthetic gene clusters derived from Aesculus-specific WGD
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key entry enzyme for reconstructing or enhancing aescin biosynthesis in heterologous hosts or medicinal plant engineering
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing CRA009101; transcriptome CRA009093; RNA-seq; metabolite profiling; WGCNA

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT-DEST1, constitutive transient expression; pESC-HIS in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early oxidative tailoring of β-amyrin scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates β-amyrin at the C-16α position
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms a decorated triterpene intermediate toward protoaescigenin/aescin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seeds, especially cotyledons near the testa
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Introduces the C-16α hydroxyl required for barrigenol-type triterpenoid formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression with AcOSC6 and AstHMGR in N. benthamiana; expression in β-amyrin-producing yeast; GC–MS/MS comparison with BfCYP716Y1 product
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AcCYP716A275 converted β-amyrin to 16α-hydroxy-β-amyrin. 2. Activity was reproduced in N. benthamiana and engineered yeast. 3. It is a BAT BGC enzyme contributing to aescin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Demonstrates CYP716A-mediated region-specific oxidation in horse chestnut BAT pathway
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful oxidative tailoring enzyme for pathway reconstruction of aescin-type triterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing CRA009101; transcriptome CRA009093; RNA-seq; WGCNA

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT-DEST1, constitutive transient expression; pESC-HIS in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early oxidative tailoring of β-amyrin scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates β-amyrin at the C-21β position
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms a decorated triterpene intermediate toward protoaescigenin/aescin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seeds, especially cotyledons near the testa
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Introduces the C-21β hydroxyl required for aescin aglycone decoration
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression with AcOSC6 and AstHMGR in N. benthamiana; expression in β-amyrin-producing yeast; GC–MS/MS comparison with GmCYP72A69 product
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AcCYP716A278 converted β-amyrin to 21β-hydroxy-β-amyrin. 2. Activity was validated in both N. benthamiana and engineered yeast. 3. It shows that C-21β oxidation in Aesculus evolved in CYP716A rather than CYP72A.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Important evolutionary finding: C-21β hydroxylation arose independently in CYP716A and CYP72A lineages
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable oxidative enzyme for synthetic biology of BAT/aescin-like molecules
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing CRA009101; transcriptome CRA009093; RNA-seq; WGCNA

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Candidate BAT BGC gene; functional relevance to seed aescin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Glycosylation/glucuronidation branch of triterpenoid saponin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucuronic acid from UDP-glucuronic acid to the C-3 hydroxyl of protoaescigenin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required glycosylation step toward aescin formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seeds, especially cotyledons near the testa
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Adds the glucuronic acid moiety essential for forming glycosylated aescin intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast substrate-feeding assay; yeast microsome enzyme assay; LC–MS product detection
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AcCSL1 glucuronosylated protoaescigenin at C-3. 2. Activity matched the known soybean CSyGT positive control. 3. It supports recruitment of CSL genes into BAT BGCs before speciation in Hippocastanoideae.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A noncanonical cellulose synthase-derived glycosyltransferase recruited into specialized metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential for assembling glycosylated triterpene saponin pathways in heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing CRA009101; transcriptome CRA009093; RNA-seq; phylogenomics

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Escherichia coli; in vitro enzyme system
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late acylation step of triterpenoid saponin modification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an acetyl group from acetyl-CoA to the C-22 hydroxyl of protoaescigenin or desacetylaescin I
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the acetylated aescin product escin Ia from desacetylaescin I
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal anti-edema and venotonic activities; C-21/C-22 acylation increases cytotoxicity according to cited pharmacodynamic studies
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seeds, especially cotyledons near the testa
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes the decisive C-22 acetylation step leading to escin Ia formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzyme assay with recombinant protein from E. coli; LC–MS; NMR confirmation of 22-O-acetylprotoaescigenin; conversion of desacetylaescin I to escin Ia
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AcBAHD6 acetylated protoaescigenin to 22-O-acetylprotoaescigenin. 2. It efficiently converted desacetylaescin I to escin Ia. 3. It is a seed-specific late-step enzyme critical for aescin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant for C-22 acetylation, not formally rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although not located in the main BGCs, it was prioritized from WGCNA and had stronger activity than AcBAHD3
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value engineering target to produce escin Ia specifically rather than less-acetylated intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing CRA009101; transcriptome CRA009093; RNA-seq; WGCNA

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Escherichia coli/in vitro recombinant enzyme assay
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Activation of hydroxycinnamate precursor for coumarin branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates caffeic acid to caffeoyl-CoA in an ATP- and CoA-dependent reaction
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor for esculetin/aesculin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Used in eye-drop formulations to alleviate asthenopia, eye pain, and diplopia
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves and flowers show highest aesculin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies caffeoyl-CoA precursor for aesculin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Purified recombinant enzyme assay monitored by spectrophotometry
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ac4CL1 converted caffeic acid to caffeoyl-CoA. 2. It is one of three functional 4CLs in horse chestnut coumarin biosynthesis. 3. It shows broad expression unlike the pericarp-biased Ac4CL2/3.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper functionally validated Ac4CL1-3, but Ac4CL2 was selected for microbial de novo aesculin production due to better catalytic performance
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful precursor-supplying enzyme for aesculin pathway engineering
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing CRA009101; transcriptome CRA009093; RNA-seq

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Escherichia coli; engineered E. coli pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Activation of hydroxycinnamate precursor for coumarin branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates caffeic acid to caffeoyl-CoA in an ATP- and CoA-dependent reaction
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms the immediate precursor used by F6'H to generate esculetin en route to aesculin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves and flowers show highest aesculin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: M9 medium with 5 g/L yeast extract for microbial production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Most efficient 4CL isoform for supplying caffeoyl-CoA and enabling de novo aesculin production in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Purified recombinant enzyme assay; kinetic comparison; deployment in engineered E. coli de novo pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ac4CL2 converted caffeic acid to caffeoyl-CoA with superior catalytic properties among tested Ac4CLs. 2. It was selected for de novo aesculin biosynthesis in E. coli. 3. It is a practical pathway entry enzyme for coumarin glycoside engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Candidate key control point among Ac4CL isoforms, not formally rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Despite pericarp-biased expression, it was preferred for microbial reconstruction due to stronger catalytic properties
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Preferred 4CL isoform for synthetic biology production of aesculin
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing CRA009101; transcriptome CRA009093; RNA-seq

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Escherichia coli/in vitro recombinant enzyme assay
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Activation of hydroxycinnamate precursor for coumarin branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates caffeic acid to caffeoyl-CoA in an ATP- and CoA-dependent reaction
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor for downstream esculetin/aesculin synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves and flowers show highest aesculin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies caffeoyl-CoA precursor for aesculin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Purified recombinant enzyme assay monitored by spectrophotometry
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ac4CL3 converted caffeic acid to caffeoyl-CoA. 2. It is a functional but not top-performing 4CL isoform in this pathway. 3. Its expression is pericarp-biased.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not used in the final reconstructed E. coli aesculin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing CRA009101; transcriptome CRA009093; RNA-seq

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Escherichia coli; engineered E. coli pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in pericarp and branch, low in flower
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Coumarin ring formation branch from hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates caffeoyl-CoA leading to formation of esculetin in the coumarin pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms the immediate aglycone precursor of aesculin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves and flowers show highest aesculin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: M9 medium with 5 g/L yeast extract for microbial production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Converts caffeoyl-CoA to esculetin and supports de novo aesculin production in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme assay in E. coli cells; LC–MS/MS product verification; use in engineered E. coli pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AcF6'H1 converted caffeoyl-CoA to esculetin. 2. It was functionally validated and chosen for de novo aesculin synthesis in E. coli. 3. It links hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA activation to coumarin aglycone formation.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Committed coumarin-forming step, not formally rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although AcF6'H2 had higher catalytic efficiency, AcF6'H1 was selected for the assembled E. coli pathway
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful coumarin-forming enzyme for microbial and plant aesculin engineering
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing CRA009101; transcriptome CRA009093; RNA-seq

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Escherichia coli/in vitro recombinant enzyme assay
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Coumarin ring formation branch from hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates caffeoyl-CoA leading to formation of esculetin in the coumarin pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate aglycone precursor of aesculin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves and flowers show highest aesculin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Efficiently converts caffeoyl-CoA to esculetin as a key aglycone-forming step in aesculin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme assay in E. coli cells; LC–MS/MS product verification; kinetic comparison
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AcF6'H2 converted caffeoyl-CoA to esculetin. 2. It showed higher catalytic efficiency than AcF6'H1. 3. It is a flower-biased coumarin-pathway enzyme.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Candidate key enzyme among F6'H isoforms, not formally rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Despite highest catalytic efficiency, not the enzyme chosen for final E. coli de novo pathway assembly
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising alternative F6'H isoform for optimized aesculin engineering
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing CRA009101; transcriptome CRA009093; RNA-seq

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Escherichia coli/in vitro recombinant enzyme assay
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pMAL-c2x or pET28a, inducible recombinant expression
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers one glucose from UDP-glucose to esculetin to form aesculin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the target metabolite aesculin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves and flowers show highest aesculin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant protein assay with esculetin and UDP-glucose; UPLC/LC–MS comparison to authentic aesculin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AcUGT84A56 formed aesculin from esculetin. 2. It is one of two validated terminal glucosyltransferases in horse chestnut. 3. It had weaker substrate affinity than AcUGT92G7.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Higher Km than AcUGT92G7 for esculetin
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative terminal glycosyltransferase for aesculin pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing CRA009101; transcriptome CRA009093; RNA-seq

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Escherichia coli; engineered E. coli pathway
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pMAL-c2x or pET28a recombinant expression; pZE-649T pathway plasmid
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers one glucose from UDP-glucose to esculetin to form aesculin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves and flowers show highest aesculin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: M9 medium with 5 g/L yeast extract for microbial production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Most effective validated terminal glucosyltransferase for aesculin production and selected for de novo E. coli biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant protein assay with esculetin and UDP-glucose; kinetic analysis; deployment in engineered E. coli pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AcUGT92G7 directly formed aesculin from esculetin. 2. It had stronger affinity for esculetin than AcUGT84A56. 3. It was selected for de novo aesculin synthesis in E. coli.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant for aesculin formation, not formally rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Km for esculetin was lower than that of AcUGT84A56, supporting pathway selection
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Preferred terminal glycosyltransferase for high-efficiency aesculin biosynthesis engineering
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing CRA009101; transcriptome CRA009093; RNA-seq
